description: I have had a great experience in 8 years of being with Bandzoogle. Strongly recommend to other music makers.
author: Ita Alexander A.
pros: Excellent for everyone regardless of specialty; but especially for musicians and those who sell music.
cons: Nothing. It is a great website builder for everyone

review_title: Bandzoogle The Great Website for you band
description: My overall experience was very positive.
author: Brian T.
pros: Bandzoogle is super easy to use and has all of the features necessary to have a feature rich website. It's a one stop shop for the working musician on a budget.
cons: Sometimes I had problems with the layout.

description: i hated it and am extremely disapoointed that i invested any money on this platform
author: marcela C.
pros: i do not enjoy this platform it is not user friendly and does not have many template options it has no ease in flow of the workspace. i am extremely disappointed this platform cost so much for such little features and customization.
cons: the workspace is not as customizable as it implied it was it does not have many template options or features that makes it difficult to use the designs in the template options are subpar
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: At least among those providers I know with easy-to-make-by-yourself sites, I consider it the most easiest and by that effective offer
author: DJ Luther T.
pros: Easy in the first place, for effectively building fast an attractive site
cons: The size of uploadable (music) files is way too small; there should be an extension for this, maybe with an upgrade

description: I have appreciated the brief time it took to learn my way around the site. Once, I became familiar with the layouts/pages, it's easy. When I've had questions, real people help is right there.
author: John, Johnny B., B.
pros: Customer service and ease of use is great!
cons: All has been running smoothly for quite some time so I don't have any 'least' likes.

review_title: Glad this is what I use!
author: Hannah D.
pros: It's very user friendly - I'm able to design a professional website completely by myself on my mobile devics.
cons: Sometimes on mobile its a bit tricky but it's still pretty good.

review_title: DO THE ZOOGLE- BEST KEPT SECRET
description: Outstanding! The site is easy to navigate through if you have a general experience. Coming from WordPress this was a hands down. The storage availability does not mean picking and choosing between your favs; upload all your best.
author: Andrea F.
pros: Ease of set-up - you will be pleasantly surprised.
cons: Wish the EPK creator was included in the monthly subscription.

description: I am a solo artist and I do everything on my own, including marketing, shipping orders, etc so for me it's very important to have tools which make my life easier and don't overwhelm me with complexity and problems. That's why I like Bandzoogle - it makes my life easier.
author: Alexandra Z.
pros: I was delighted to know that there is a platform dedicated entirely to musicians, therefore optimised for such content. I like how easy it is to set up everything even for a person who is generally not inclined to deal with such tasks. Every function that I ever needed for my website is there and easy to use. The fact that one gets such a service together with a domain for this price is definitely a treat.
cons: So far, there were just two issues that I have found that need improvement. Backdating posts seem to trigger some bugs, but the support immediately addressed the issue, so I assume it will be fixed quickly. The other thing is that there was no option to add a tracking number for the shipped merch orders. I already made a note of this issue so I hope it will be resolved soon.

review_title: Disgraceful company who discriminated against a gay person
description: Terrible. Don't use them. Use another service.
author: Alexander B.
pros: It is easy to use, very easy to be banned from their system for no reason. It seems their staff don't agree with gay people using the platform.
cons: Their customer support and discrimination against me, which led to distress.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Excellent experience and will continue to use them!
author: Nadia A.
pros: Easy to use website, especially if you're not tech savvy.
cons: Need more options to embed a video with text. But that is honestly not a big deal.

description: Very pleased with my overall experience with Breederoo.
author: Marsha D.
pros: I like the support team answering on a timely basis
cons: I wish we could add videos to our site pages

description: I have begun using Bandzoogle as the final destination for the main branding and presentation of my final products. I have found that offering teasers and songs on other social media sites that direct them to Bandzoogle have helped me brand better.
author: Brian G.
pros: What I like the most about Bandzoogle is the easily customizable templates and the instant gratification I get from seeing my recent edits and changes live without having to wait for them to take effect. Bandzoogle's layout and functions are very easy to use and can the design aspects allows me to create a unique look for my site. I find Bandzoogle to be seamless in it's integration with social media and streaming sites.
cons: I sometimes find it a little confusing as I am editing content to know how to move sections around on a page. The new page creation process is great, the Add a Feature process is easy. Once I have done that and want to move that section to another portion of the page, I have found it a little confusing to follow the arrows or know where the section will end up on the page.

review_title: Great all around tool for musicians
description: I love how much easier it is to manage our site and online needs as a band in ONE tool instead of 50 different one. Love this product.
author: Reid G.
pros: While you might be able to find other tools that do one part of the system a little better, Bandzoogle has a pretty solid set of tools all in one place. It is the easiest way for us to manage our website with most features we need for all aspects of our band.
cons: There are a few tools I'd like to see have a few more configuration options, but overall what you get is great and acceptable. The value of the whole system far surpasses nitpicking on any one part of it.

description: It’s a great tool. Much easier than anything else I’ve used. And the features give me options to update content weekly, instead of a single page that never needs updating like other products I have used.
author: David W.
pros: I am able to keep all our info and communications in one place. The blog feature allows us to create post in one place that we can then share to social media. No more recreating post for every service, just one post to share! And it drives fans to your website where they may stay for a bit and find a live show coming up, or buy a shirt.
cons: A little more flexibility with text formatting would be nice. For example, if I could change the text color on the title of blogs, that would help it stand out against the background.

review_title: probably the best music web host, if you're a musician and trying to do it all yourself.
description: It's great for musicians with public presences. All the important things to do can potentially be done through Bandzoogle.
author: darol A.
pros: Most of the features are directly useful to artists and especially musicians and can be accessed and are usable.
cons: the music file download feature is super-important, seems to be well-explained, but trouble-shooting seems a bit opaque. everything worked really well until the last.

review_title: Slick and easy web design with capacity to sell merchandise and downloads
author: Sharon W.
pros: We wanted to have access to e-sales, so downloading of music files was the primary criteria, which Bandzoogle offers at the reasonable price. We were able to set up a nice web site and store that also included players and some space for commentary and photos. It is a convenient package compared to other solutions we had tried.
cons: The only complaint I had in setting up the Bandzoogle site was that the menus sometimes seemed to loop me back instead of saving and progressing on - this showed my inexperience with automated design, and reminded me why I always hated the term "intuitive" in computer usage. However, it also gave the Bandzoogle tech support people a chance to shine.

description: My experience with Bandzoogle is excellent. I wouldn't want to have it any other way. Their customer support is also wonderful. I would most definitely recommend Bandzoogle to everyone.
author: Shawn M.
pros: I like the face that it is user friendly. You can set up an efficient website within a few minutes.
cons: My only con is they don't have a wide range of colours to choose from for your website

review_title: Best Support and Service for building your own website. Easy and Inexpensive
description: Our experience has been tremendous, and our fans and family now have a hub location to follow and be aware of all upcoming events and releases and playdates. Also a place to show a gallery od photos and music and merchandise offerings. Launching email campaigns has been so much easier and effective.Bandzoogle has made a sometimes difficult necessity easy and palatable
author: Michael C.
pros: Best Support and Service for building your own website. Easy and Inexpensive
cons: No complaints,.. Bandzoogle has allowed us to build a much better website for our fans.

review_title: Bandzoogle is Ideal for Musicians, Artist, Entertainers
description: Amazing and wonderful
author: Derrick M.
pros: Easy to use and excellent customer support
cons: It does offer downloads to mobile devices like itunes, spotify.

description: Great! It took a white to find the right template for my style of music, but once I found what I liked, and tweaked it, so I was happy, it is great. Easy to update when I want to add new songs and videos.
author: Ro H.
pros: Easy to use and reliable. Once I found the right template, and tweaked it the way I wanted it to look, all was good. It’s easy to update my song list and to add new videos and recordings.
cons: It took some time to get the right template and edit it to get it just right.

author: Bill N.
pros: Support... any strange things happen, and they are there
cons: Just normal website building frustrations... not your fault

description: It has been great to have a central platform where venues, bookers, and media can access information, photos and videos and other news about the band. Much more effective than trying to do the same with social media
author: Tony S.
pros: It is tailored to the features that an independent music artist needs and has done a lot to make our band look very professional and serious about our music.
cons: The email feature and administration is a little difficult to find and keep on top of unless you check it regularly. Would nice to be able to send incoming emails to multiple email clients.

review_title: Best product in my music business
description: This company is by far the best customer service in my whole music business. They are super friendly and reply very fast. And this has been my continued experience for three years now.
author: Paul J R.
pros: This product is a really fair price. Its very easy to use and looks very professional
cons: Absolutely nothing. It's perfect for my needs

description: Customer service has been outstanding and my website looks good and is reliable.
author: Patrick N.
pros: The ability to customize my website with music, photos and video content.
cons: Waiting for analytics.....I wish there was more data and in real time.

description: I love it. As I already mentioned, it makes totally autonomous and it looks ace!
author: Hugh R.
pros: As a new artist, and totally zero in Website construction skills, the Bandzoogle software allowed me to single handily create a professional-looking Website I am proud to tell the world about!
cons: There are a few things that are on the learning curve, but, like all things, the more you do it, the easier it becomes.
